title: Garrison shell UX overhaul (refine-within-identity)

e2e.summary: 4 passed on a fresh sandbox server (committed spec: routes clean, tile summaries, search filter, status tone dots)

review.summary: escapeHtml scope adequate for innerHTML sub-line; skeleton classes defined; reduced-motion + focus-visible accessibility-positive

codexReview.notes: Round 1: 3 real findings, all applied (reuseExistingServer:false to guarantee .next-e2e isolation; global-setup warm-up now hard-fails; PageSkeleton widths responsive). Round 2: clean approve.

codexPwTest.notes: Codex sandbox cannot launch Chromium (bootstrap_check_in MachPortRendezvousServer permission denied) - the documented environment-block class, not a product defect. Static fallback: its r2 review inspected the committed spec + diff; Claude's own committed 4-test Playwright spec independently drives the same acceptance and passes.

designAudit.by: claude vision over live screenshots (desktop + 390px mobile)

notes: Refine-within-identity per plan: skeleton loading states (PageSkeleton), Compose tile sub-lines now show the Fitting's summary (escaped, word-boundary clipped), sidebar Views tone dots, focus-visible/selection/reduced-motion polish, plus e2e infra hardening (.next-e2e isolation, warm-up, committed shell-overhaul.spec.ts).
